message:
fix: memory leak in addrinfo allocation - use contiguous layout

build_addrinfo_v4/v6 previously allocated ai_addr (sockaddr_in/in6)
separately via Box::new. The recent freeaddrinfo change removed the
code that freed ai_addr, causing memory leaks.

Fix by using contiguous allocation (struct with both addrinfo and
sockaddr embedded), matching glibc's behavior. Now freeaddrinfo can
correctly free with a single free() call, which works for both our
allocations and any host-allocated addrinfo structures.

Introduced ContiguousAddrinfoV4 and ContiguousAddrinfoV6 repr(C)
structs to ensure proper layout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
